revision.text:
[[ja:長者山新羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chōjazan Shinra Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Chojasan Shinra-jinja honden.jpg |location = {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, [[Hachinohe City]], Chōja 1-6-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|40.502833|N|141.491028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} (Shinra Saburō Minamoto no Yoshimitsu no Mikoto) |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 6 (1678) |Main Hall = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternative name = Chōjazan |Festivals = |Main Festival = August 2 |Rituals = Enburi (February 17–20)
Hachinohe Sansha Taisai (July 31–August 4)
Kagami-style horseback polo (August 2) |Map={{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=10.2|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=27.7|label=Chōjazan Shinra Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|qq=}}|location = {{ill|Chōja|qid=Q11653567|1=Chōja}} |deity = {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Chōjasan Shinra Shrine |image = [[File:Chojasansinrajinja2.JPG|250px]] | location = 1-6-10 Chōja, {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|qid=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe|1=Hachinohe}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|40|30|10.2|N|141|29|27.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
{{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Shinra Saburō Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} | Shrine Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine (旧県社) | Foundation = {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 6 (1678) | Main hall style = Irimoya-zukuri | alternate_name = Chōjasan | Annual festival = August 2 | Sacred rites = Enburi (February 17–20)
Hachinohe Sansha Grand Festival (July 31–August 4)
Kagami-style horseback polo (August 2) | Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=30|lat_sec=10.2|lon_deg=141|lon_min=29|lon_sec=27.7|label=Chōjasan Shinra Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|qq=}} }} '''Chōjazan Shinra Shrine''' (長者山新羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located atop Mount Chōja in {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|qid=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe|1=Hachinohe}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}. The shrine holds the status of a former prefectural shrine and is renowned for its association with the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|qid=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|1=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} and {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}, both designated as [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]]. == Lede2 == '''Chōjazan Shinra Shrine''' (長者山新羅神社, Chōjazan Shinra Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located on Chōjazan Mountain in {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|qid=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}. It holds the Rank of a former prefectural shrine and is widely revered alongside {{ill|Kushihiki Hachimangū|en|Kushihiki Hachimangū|qid=Q11544667|lt=Kushihiki Hachimangū}} within the city. The shrine is renowned for hosting the {{ill|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|en|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|qid=Q5637073|lt=Hachinohe Sansha Taisai}} and the {{ill|Enburi|ja|えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}, both designated as Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties. == Deities == ; Main deities * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (素佐嗚尊) * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Shinra Saburō Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} (新羅三郎源義光命) Other deities are enshrined in a {{ill|Saijin|ja|相殿|lt=shared sanctuary (Aiden-shin)|fr|Saijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭神}}. == History == Chōjazan has been a site of rituals since ancient times. The shrine traces its origins to a hall called "Gion," enshrining {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu}}, established by the first {{ill|Hachinohe Domain|en|Hachinohe Domain|qid=Q7884927|lt=Hachinohe}} lord, {{ill|Nanbu Nobufusa|ja|南部直房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nambu Nobufusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8513884}}. The shrine was officially founded in {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} 6 (1678) by the second domain lord, {{ill|Nanbu Naomasa|ja|南部直政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nambu Naomasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8513878}}, who enshrined Shinra Saburō Minamoto no Yoshimitsu, a distant ancestor of the Nambu clan, as the guardian deity of the domain. It was initially called "Sanshadō" or "Kokūzōdō." Over time, Chōjazan Shinra Shrine became the central guardian shrine of the {{ill|Hachinohe Domain|en|Hachinohe Domain|qid=Q7884927|lt=Hachinohe Domain}}. Shrine construction and maintenance were directly overseen by the domain. The current shrine buildings date back to {{ill|1827|en|1827|qid=Q7008|lt=1827}} (Bunsei 10), when they were reconstructed by the eighth domain lord, {{ill|Nambu Nobumasa|ja|南部信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nambu Nobumasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8513895}}. The shrine grounds include a sakura equestrian training area where horseback {{ill|Polo|en|polo|qid=Q134211|lt=polo}} (打毬) was performed during festivals. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869}} (Meiji 2), during the [[:en:Shinbutsu Bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the shrine's name was changed to "Shinra Shrine," and it was designated as a {{Interlanguage link|District Shrine|qid=Q134917316}}. The shrine hosted {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} during his visit to Tōhoku, where Kagami-style horseback polo was presented. It later achieved {{Interlanguage link|prefectural shrine|qid=Q90139226}} status and was renamed to its current name Chōjazan Shinra Shrine in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51). == Festivals == ; Enburi ''' {{ill|Enburi|ja|えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} : Held February 17–20, this traditional folk dance ritual celebrates the agricultural New Year. Performers from Hachinohe and surrounding areas visit the shrine and then parade through the city, performing at various locations. Enburi is an important form of {{ill|Yoshuku|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku|qq=}} and was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979}}. ; festival : Held August 2, the festival includes a sacred horseback {{ill|Polo|en|polo|qid=Q134211|lt=polo}} event in the sakura training area. The event features two types of polo: "Kachi-dakyū" (played on foot) and "Kiba-dakyū" (horseback polo). The latter is a Kagami-style martial art practice introduced during the Edo period to train samurai. ; Hachinohe Sansha Grand Festival {{Main|Hachinohe Sansha Taisai|qq=}} : Celebrated from July 31 to August 4, this grand festival includes the procession of three shrines' portable shrines and floats, with the cooperation of {{ill|Ogami Shrine|ja|龗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimmei Shrine (Hachinohe)|ja|神明宮 (八戸市廿六日町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei Shrine (Hachinohe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === The current main hall and worship hall were constructed between {{ill|1826|en|1826|qid=Q7004|lt=1826}}–{{ill|1827|en|1827|qid=Q7008|lt=1827}} (Bunsei 9–10). The main hall is built in the Irimoya-zukuri style with three-by-two bays, featuring vibrant decorative carvings. The worship hall, unique in its three-bay depth, is also in the Irimoya-zukuri style. Both buildings were designated as Aomori Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991}} (Heisei 3). == Cultural Properties == === National Designation === * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** ''' {{ill|Enburi|ja|えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} === Aomori Prefecture Designation === * Tangible Cultural Property (Architecture) ** Shinra Shrine Main Hall and Worship Hall * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kagami-style horseback polo == References == * 『神道大辞典』 (Shintō Encyclopedia), Heibonsha, 1939 (reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0 * 『神社名鑑』 (Shrine Directory), Association of Shinto Shrines, 1963 == External Links == * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/18.htm Official website of Chōjazan Shinra Shrine (Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association)] * [http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/ Cultural Properties of Aomori Prefecture] * [http://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/shiryo/guidebook/top.html Hachinohe Cultural Properties Guidebook] [[ja:長者山新羅神社]] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/18.htm Chōjazan Shinra Shrine] (Aomori Shrine Office) * [http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/ Aomori Cultural Properties] (Aomori Prefecture Cultural Properties Division) * [http://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/shiryo/guidebook/top.html Hachinohe Cultural Properties Guidebook] * [http://www.at-ml.jp/72215/ Official Website of Chōjazan Shinra Shrine] {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Shinto side|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|長者山新羅神社|version=102205135|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:長者山新羅神社]] {{Commons category|Chojasan Shinra-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11653578}} [[Category:Shrines in Hachinohe City]] [[Category:八戸藩]] [[Category:源義光]] [[Category:青森県の旧県社]] [[Category:青森県重宝]]
